title: Lightweight Instant Closet Rack
summary: A simple portable 1x2 ladder-frame rack with rubber feet — freestanding closet or entryway hanging storage that needs no wall mounting at all.
description:
This rack is built entirely from lightweight 1x2 stock in a simple ladder-frame shape, designed to be freestanding rather than wall-mounted — set it in an empty closet, a bedroom corner, or an entryway and it's ready to hang clothes on without a single screw going into a wall. Build the two side ladder frames first, joining the uprights with evenly spaced rungs, then join the two frames together with cross supports at the top and bottom.

The hanging rod sits at the top, spanning between the two side frames, and rubber feet go on the bottom of each leg to protect flooring and add a little grip against the rack sliding around. **This is intentionally lightweight and portable** — it's not built to carry the load of a permanent closet system, and it's meant to be moved, not anchored.

A genuinely fast build with almost no waste, this is a good project for anyone needing quick extra hanging storage — a dorm room, a guest room closet with no existing rod, or a seasonal rack that gets put away when it's not needed.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four leg uprights at 60", six ladder rungs at 16" and four top/bottom cross supports at 48" from the 1x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to eight 8 ft 1x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the two side ladder frames
steps.body: Attach the ladder rungs evenly spaced between each pair of leg uprights with 1-1/4" screws and glue, building two identical side frames.

steps.title: Join the frames together
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom cross supports between the two side frames, forming the freestanding rack shape.

steps.title: Install the rod and feet
steps.body: Attach the rod flanges to the top of the frame and install the closet rod. Attach rubber feet to the bottom of each leg.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Portable freestanding closet rack with hanging rod
